CheckTitle: Ensure agent identities do not hold standing privileged roles
Description: Detects agent identities that hold a permanent (standing) assignment to a high-privilege directory role instead of a time-limited, just-in-time entitlement.
Risk: A standing privileged role gives an agent continuous high-impact access that persists even when the agent is idle or compromised. Temporary access granted without an expiry becomes permanent by default, widening the blast radius of a stolen credential and violating least-privilege for AI agents.

Remediation.Recommendation: Remove permanent privileged role assignments from agent identities. Grant privileged access as time-limited, just-in-time entitlements scoped to the specific task (for example, PIM-eligible assignments with activation limits). Prefer narrowly scoped, resource-specific roles over standing directory-wide roles.

SourceNotes: Detection reads active directory role assignments (roleManagement/directory/roleAssignments) whose principal is an agent identity, which is available on all Entra tiers. Converting to just-in-time eligible assignments (the recommended remediation) requires Microsoft Entra ID P2 / PIM. Grounded in Microsoft's 'Least privilege for AI agents' guidance (2026): temporary access without an expiry becomes permanent.
